title: salvia lavandulifolia var. vellerea
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia var. vellerea is a variant of the Salvia lavandulifolia plant, commonly known as 'Sweet Lavender Sage'. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as camphor and borneol. The medicinal properties of Salvia lavandulifolia var. vellerea have been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin irritations, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its health benefits.
